stub: He is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background.
constituency: He is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: He tends to vote in accordance with the instructions of his party, even when that conflicts with the wishes of his constituents.
substantive: He supports increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, he regularly explains how his decisions help to promote the national interest.
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said:  “Elections are important because they force me as a politician to listen and respond to the views of voters.”
text: He is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background. He is from a constituency that is not your constituency. He tends to vote in accordance with the instructions of his party, even when that conflicts with the wishes of his constituents. He supports increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, he regularly explains how his decisions help to promote the national interest. Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said:  “Elections are important because they force me as a politician to listen and respond to the views of voters.”
